title: Emergent Capabilities
concept: Emergence occurs when scaling past a threshold unlocks sudden, qualitative abilities (like multi-step reasoning) that smaller models perform at random chance.

exercises.prompt: What does 'emergence' mean when talking about Large Language Models?

options.text: Sudden, qualitative abilities unlocked by scaling.
Rules manually coded by human programmers.

exercises.explain: Emergent capabilities are defined by sudden breakthroughs in performance that only happen when models are scaled up to very large sizes.

cards.title: The Power of More
cards.body: Have you ever tried to lift a heavy couch? One person can't budge it at all. Two people might not move it either. But when a third person joins, the couch suddenly lifts off the ground! In AI, adding more size and data can suddenly unlock brand-new abilities that smaller models couldn't do at all.

cards.title: What is Emergence?
cards.body: Emergent capabilities are new, unexpected skills that appear in AI models only after they grow past a certain size. Below this threshold, the model performs at random chance. Once crossed, the model suddenly gains a qualitative new skill, like solving math riddles or coding.

cards.title: The Reading Breakthrough
cards.body: Imagine a kid learning to read. Recognizing individual letters doesn't help them read a full book at first—they just see random letters. But once they learn enough words, a sudden breakthrough happens: they can suddenly read and understand entire stories! The ability to comprehend stories 'emerges' all at once.

cards.title: The Logic Puzzle Jump
cards.body: A small model with 1 billion parameters might score 0% on a complex multi-step logic puzzle, guessing randomly. But when scaled up to a 100-billion-parameter model trained on the exact same data, it suddenly scores 70%. The ability to reason through steps emerged from scale!

cards.title: Myth: Slow and Steady Progress
cards.body: People think AI models get slightly better at every task as they grow. Actually, for many complex tasks, models show zero improvement for a long time, looking completely lost. Then, suddenly, they cross a size threshold and the ability appears out of nowhere!

cards.title: Gradual vs. Sudden
cards.body: Compare how a model's performance changes on basic tasks versus emergent tasks as the model size scales up.

left.text: Show smooth, gradual improvement. The model gets slightly better with every small increase in size.

right.text: Flatline at zero performance, followed by a sudden vertical jump in capability once a size threshold is crossed.

exercises.prompt: How do AI models perform on an emergent task *before* they reach the threshold size?

options.text: They perform with near-perfect accuracy from the start.
They perform at near random chance, showing no real skill.
They perform slightly better than human experts.
They refuse to answer any questions at all.

exercises.explain: When a model is too small, its performance on an emergent task is no better than random guessing (0% or close to it). It only improves after reaching a critical size.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Emergent abilities develop gradually and steadily at every stage of an AI's growth.
exercises.explain: Emergent abilities are defined by sudden breakthroughs, not smooth and gradual improvements. Smaller models perform at random chance until the threshold is met.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of AI emergence.
exercises.sentence: Emergent abilities only appear after an AI model grows past a specific size threshold.
exercises.explain: Below a certain size threshold, an AI model cannot perform the complex task at all. Once it crosses this size threshold, the ability suddenly emerges.

exercises.prompt: Which of these AI systems shows an 'emergent capability'?

options.text: System A: Starts at 10% accuracy, and slowly goes up to 15%, then 20% as it grows.
System B: Scores 0% accuracy for a long time, then suddenly jumps to 80% accuracy.
System C: Scores 100% accuracy at all sizes without any training.

exercises.explain: System B shows emergence because it suddenly jumps from zero ability to a high success rate, whereas System A shows a gradual, predictable improvement.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes how 'emergent capabilities' show up in large language models?

options.text: They improve slowly and steadily with every single parameter added.
They appear suddenly as a breakthrough after a model passes a certain size threshold.
They are pre-programmed by human engineers using specific rule templates.
They only happen in smaller models because they are easier to train.

exercises.explain: Emergent capabilities are like magic tricks that only appear when a model is scaled past a certain size threshold. Below that size, performance on complex tasks looks like random guessing. Above it, the model suddenly 'gets it' and performance climbs rapidly.
